subject: Geography
title: Cave Art
description: A micro-lesson that makes Cave Art usable.

hook.text: The oldest known cave art is over 45,000 years old—a painting of a pig in an Indonesian cave.

buildup.text: Lascaux in France holds 600 paintings of horses, bulls, and deer from 17,000 years ago.

discovery.text: Artists used charcoal, ochre, and their own hands to paint by flickering torchlight.

twist.text: Lascaux was closed to the public in 1963 because visitors' breath was destroying the paintings.

climax.text: These paintings survived ice ages and millennia—but not the carbon dioxide of tourists.

punchline.text: Art that survived 17,000 years couldn't survive 20 years of visitors.

quiz.question: Why was Lascaux Cave closed to visitors?
options: Visitors' breath was damaging the ancient paintings
